documentation.readme: A plugin-based scanner that aids security researchers in identifying issues with several CMS.
usage_sections: Joomla (version enumeration and interesting URLs only)
Moodle (plugin & theme very limited, watch out)
allows simultaneous scanning of multiple sites
p -- Plugin checks: Performs several thousand HTTP requests and returns a
t -- Theme checks: As above, but for themes.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the web category. Fits when the engagement needs: A plugin-based scanner that aids security researchers in identifying issues with several CMS.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: A plugin-based scanner that aids security researchers in identifying issues with several CMS.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: apt-get install python-pip; pip install droopescan. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `computer:~/droopescan$ droopescan scan drupal -u $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL -t 32`. Further invocations from upstream: `Default changelog file - $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL`; `Default admin - $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL`. A plugin-based scanner that aids security researchers in identifying issues with several CMS.

name: droopescan
summary: A plugin-based scanner that aids security researchers in identifying issues with several CMS.

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need web, use droopescan because A plugin-based scanner that aids security researchers in identifying issues with several CMS.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `computer:~/droopescan$ droopescan scan drupal -u $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL -t 32` as documented upstream for droopescan.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Default changelog file - $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL` as documented upstream for droopescan.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Default admin - $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL` as documented upstream for droopescan.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `views $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL` as documented upstream for droopescan.
When chaining web tools, pair droopescan with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `droopescan --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
